skill.name: nano-banana-2
skill.description: Generate still images with Google Nano Banana 2 through the local RunComfy CLI. The skill documents model selection, input options, prompting patterns, output formats, pricing-sensitive resolution choices, and RunComfy security notes.
skill.summary: Generate Nano Banana 2 images through RunComfy with practical prompt and output controls.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many patterns because this single Markdown skill documents CLI commands, RunComfy URLs, a relative documentation link, and token storage. Manual review found no prompt injection attempt, hidden exfiltration, or malicious control flow; the confirmed risks are intended external CLI execution, outbound RunComfy API access, optional web grounding, and API-token handling. Publish with a medium-risk warning for network use and third-party CLI execution.

medium_findings.title: RunComfy CLI Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The external command findings are mostly true positives because the skill instructs the agent to run the local RunComfy CLI. This is intended behavior for image generation, but it executes third-party tooling and writes generated files to a user-selected output directory.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Markdown clearly documents concrete RunComfy CLI invocations. The commands are fixed endpoint calls with JSON input, so the risk is real but aligned with the skill purpose.

medium_findings.title: Documented Outbound Network Access
medium_findings.description: The network findings are true positives. The skill sends prompts to RunComfy model API endpoints and downloads generated outputs from RunComfy domains; optional web grounding can add external context processing.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced endpoints and download domains are explicit. No hidden callback or unrelated exfiltration endpoint was found in the reviewed file.

medium_findings.title: RunComfy Token Storage and Secret Handling
medium_findings.description: The hidden-file finding is a true positive because RunComfy login stores an API token under the user home configuration directory. The skill states owner-only permissions and an environment-token alternative, but users must still treat the token as a secret.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token storage path and environment variable are explicitly documented. The risk is moderate because it is normal credential handling, not confirmed credential theft.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Finding Is a Documentation Link
low_findings.description: The path traversal alert at the sibling skill reference is a false positive. It is a Markdown relative link to documentation, not file access or runtime path construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a Markdown link using a relative path. There is no code that opens, writes, or traverses local filesystem paths at that location.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts are false positives. The referenced lines describe the skill, input fields, anti-patterns, and exit-code documentation; no hashing, encryption, or signature code is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic operation on the flagged lines. The scanner appears to have matched text fragments in Markdown prose and tables.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alerts are false positives. The referenced text describes model routing and usage guidance, not host enumeration, OS probing, or system discovery commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are prose and model-selection guidance. No commands or logic for discovering local system details were found there.

dangerous_patterns.description: The external command findings are mostly true positives because the skill instructs the agent to run the local RunComfy CLI. This is intended behavior for image generation, but it executes third-party tooling and writes generated files to a user-selected output directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The Markdown clearly documents concrete RunComfy CLI invocations. The commands are fixed endpoint calls with JSON input, so the risk is real but aligned with the skill purpose.

content.user_title: Generate Nano Banana 2 Images on RunComfy
content.value_statement: Teams need fast image drafts without rebuilding prompts for every aspect ratio or resolution. This skill turns requests into RunComfy Nano Banana 2 jobs with practical controls and prompt guidance.
actual_capabilities: Runs the RunComfy Nano Banana 2 text-to-image endpoint through the local CLI.
Documents supported fields including prompt, image count, seed, aspect ratio, resolution, and output format.
Provides prompt guidance for subject-first descriptions, quoted image text, stable seeds, and style control.
Supports common output planning for drafts, vertical posts, wide images, and higher-resolution finals.
Explains when to use optional web grounding for real entities or current-event imagery.
Lists RunComfy exit codes and troubleshooting references for failed jobs.
limitations: It requires the RunComfy CLI, a RunComfy account, and valid authentication.
It generates still images only through this endpoint, not video or image edits.
Each request is limited to the model and option ranges documented by RunComfy.
Web search, 2K, and 4K settings can add cost, latency, or both.

use_cases.title: Draft Social Campaign Visuals
use_cases.description: Create several fast thumbnail concepts before selecting one for refinement.

use_cases.title: Create Product Card Concepts
use_cases.description: Generate clean product imagery with quoted in-image text and controlled framing.

use_cases.title: Prototype Editorial Images
use_cases.description: Use web-grounded context when prompts reference current events or real entities.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Image Draft
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one straightforward image.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 1K square Nano Banana 2 image of a clean studio product scene for this concept: [describe concept].

prompt_templates.title: Batch Concept Variants
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare several options before choosing a direction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create four 0.5K vertical Nano Banana 2 drafts for [campaign or post]. Keep the subject consistent and vary composition.

prompt_templates.title: Typography-Focused Asset
prompt_templates.scenario: Design an image that contains readable text.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 16:9 image with the exact text "[text]" placed [location]. Use [style] and keep the text clear.

prompt_templates.title: Seeded Final Refinement
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced refinement after selecting a concept.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 2K final using seed [seed]. Keep the selected composition, improve [details], and preserve the original framing.

output_examples.input: A request for four vertical wellness brand concepts with readable headline text.
output: Four draft images are generated in a vertical layout.
The prompt uses quoted headline text for better typography.
The response identifies the output directory and notes the chosen resolution.

output_examples.input: A request for a final 16:9 product hero image using a selected seed.
output: One higher-resolution image is generated with the requested seed.
The framing and subject remain consistent for review.
The user receives a concise note about model settings used.

best_practices: Start with 0.5K or 1K drafts, then promote the selected concept to 2K or 4K.
Quote exact in-image text and specify placement, font style, and contrast.
Enable web search only when the prompt needs current or real-world context.
anti_patterns: Do not request unsupported aspect ratios, custom resolution values, or more than four images.
Do not mix many conflicting visual styles in the same prompt.
Do not use this text-to-image endpoint for subject-preserving image edits.

faq.question: Does this skill require a RunComfy account?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses the RunComfy CLI, so authentication is required before generation.

faq.question: Can it edit an existing image?
faq.answer: No. This skill targets the text-to-image endpoint. Use an edit-specific skill for image edits.

faq.question: How many images can it generate at once?
faq.answer: The documented endpoint supports up to four images per request.

faq.question: Can it create images with text inside them?
faq.answer: Yes, but the prompt should quote the exact characters and describe placement clearly.

faq.question: Does web search run by default?
faq.answer: No. Web grounding is optional and should be enabled only when the prompt needs current context.

faq.question: Is this compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
